# GitHub Repository Configuration

## Badges and Status

Add these badges to your main README.md for professional appearance:

```markdown
![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/license/yourusername/H2Char)
![GitHub release (latest by date)](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/yourusername/H2Char)
![GitHub last commit](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/yourusername/H2Char)
![GitHub issues](https://img.shields.io/github/issues/yourusername/H2Char)
![GitHub pull requests](https://img.shields.io/github/issues-pr/yourusername/H2Char)
![GitHub Pages](https://img.shields.io/badge/GitHub-Pages-blue)
```

## Repository Settings

### Recommended GitHub Settings

1. **Description**: "H2Char: Advanced 3D Character Motion Retargeting System"
2. **Topics**: motion-retargeting, 3d-animation, character-animation, computer-graphics, deep-learning
3. **Website**: https://yourusername.github.io/H2Char
4. **Enable Discussions**: For community engagement
5. **Enable Issues**: For bug reports and feature requests
6. **Enable Wiki**: For extended documentation

### GitHub Pages Settings

1. Go to Repository Settings → Pages
2. Source: GitHub Actions
3. Custom domain (optional): Set if using custom domain
4. Enforce HTTPS: Enable for security

## Social Preview

Add a social preview image (1280×640px) named `social-preview.png` in the root directory for better sharing on social media.

## Community Guidelines

### Code of Conduct

We expect all contributors to:
- Be respectful and inclusive
- Provide constructive feedback
- Help maintain a positive environment

### Recognition

Contributors will be recognized in:
- Release notes
- Contributor list
- Project documentation

## Release Management

### Versioning

Follow semantic versioning: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes
- MINOR: New features (backward compatible)
- PATCH: Bug fixes

### Release Checklist

Before each release:
- [ ] Update version in config.json
- [ ] Update CHANGELOG.md
- [ ] Run all tests
- [ ] Update documentation
- [ ] Create GitHub release

## Security

### Reporting Security Issues

If you discover a security vulnerability, please report it privately to:
- Email: security@example.com
- Do not create public issues for security vulnerabilities

### Dependencies

Regularly update dependencies and monitor for security vulnerabilities using:
- GitHub Dependabot
- Snyk vulnerability scanning

## Support

### Getting Help

- Check documentation first
- Search existing issues
- Join community discussions
- Contact maintainers for urgent issues

### Commercial Support

For commercial support and consulting, contact: support@example.com
